work.title: The Kink Education Code of Conduct (KECC), version 1
work.creator: v. 1 KECC Collective
work.scope: historically situated educator and producer ethics framework

entries.reason: Complete section-level substantive source; concepts manually paraphrased and bounded.
All pages reviewed for visible date, FAQ, adoption limits, and full-version crosscheck; raw layout and repeated text excluded.
Collective authorship and first-version modeling rationale retained; individual names and navigation excluded.
Used only to verify attribution, change notice, ShareAlike, licence-link, and no-additional-restrictions metadata.
Linked legal code reviewed solely to validate ShareAlike-compatible output metadata; legal prose excluded.

word_count_method: Unicode regex [\w\u2019'-]+ over CORPUS.md; whitespace count recorded separately
category_scope_note: All categories describe version-1 educator and producer ethics proposals. They supply no current-consensus, legal, certification, central-enforcement, medical, technique, or safety conclusion.

evidence_scope.retained_consent: capacity-aware informed explicit or enthusiastic continuous noncoerced agreement and visible classroom modeling
evidence_scope.retained_student_demo: power differential, preference order, advance negotiation, anti-pressure controls, opt-out, inclusive selection, aftercare planning, and producer support
evidence_scope.retained_competence: topic-specific expertise, qualification or professional-review disclosure, personal-opinion disclosure, and respect for individual risk profiles
evidence_scope.retained_inclusion: nondiscrimination, pronouns, body and ability access, learning needs, diverse governance, reporting routes, and response staffing
evidence_scope.retained_accountability: feedback and repair, contacts, circles, conflict awareness, multiple routes, diversity, and pattern memory
evidence_scope.retained_boundaries: professional student relationships, formal policies, power discussion, mentoring boundaries, and no status exchange
evidence_scope.retained_vetting: bidirectional, multi-source, contextual vetting without safety clearance
evidence_scope.retained_disclosure_privacy: injury and consent-incident histories, non-retaliation, need-to-know handling, informed choice, privacy, proportionality, and unresolved tradeoffs
evidence_scope.excluded: individual names, identifier trivia, kink examples as technique, exact portable time thresholds, medical or legal procedure, investigation or sanction procedure, current-consensus claims, certification, enforcement, and safety proof

qa_derivation_policy.allowed: version-scoped role distinctions, consent modeling, demonstration power and safeguards, competence and limit disclosure, risk autonomy, inclusion and accessibility, accountability structure, educator-student boundaries, bidirectional vetting, incident disclosure, and privacy tradeoffs
qa_derivation_policy.forbidden: individual member, affiliation, contact, URL, page title, section order, date, hash, licence boilerplate, named outside organization, practice example, exact threshold, current consensus, legal rule, certification, enforcement, investigation result, sanction, consent proof, or safety proof recall; any operational kink, demonstration, medical, legal, investigation, aftercare, or response procedure

sampling_guidance.source_weighting: one KECC-v1 source-family document, not one document per webpage, section, role, commitment, or disposition
sampling_guidance.recommended_unit: chunks that retain version-1/2019 status, the responsible role, relevant rationale or power context, and no-certification or no-safety-proof boundary
sampling_guidance.avoid: isolating an aspirational commitment as evidence of compliance; converting a disclosure prompt into a clearance checklist; presenting a contact as enforcement; separating disclosure from privacy; or turning examples and time suggestions into technique or safe-harbor rules

document_disjoint_requirement: Assign the KECC homepage, full-version HTML, PDF, this Markdown, and every derivative to one source-family split before chunking or derivation.
protected_split_requirement: Exclude QA, trainer, training-projection, evaluation, validation, holdout, OOD, shadow, benchmark, probe, dataset, sealed-holdout, and protected-QA documents from every training layer.
